author: g5n-dev
title: Command Injection via Shell Metacharacter Fallback in tool_runner.py
notes: The shell metacharacter fallback in resolve_command_argv() is intentional design, not a vulnerability. The function exists to run user-configured external tools (like Roslyn) that may require shell features (pipes, env expansion). The claimed attack vector (attacker controls DESLOPPIFY_CSHARP_ROSLYN_CMD env var) presupposes the attacker already has code execution in the user's environment — setting env vars IS code execution. An attacker with env var control can trivially use PATH, LD_PRELOAD, etc. This is a local CLI tool, not a server; the threat model is invalid. S059 made a vaguer version of the same claim and was tagged REVIEW_SPAM.
